* **Total Data Points**:

* **Previous Use**:
  * Genome Aggregation Database (gnomAD): Yes. The text mentions that gnomAD will be compared, supplemented, or integrated with other ongoing large populational sequencing efforts.
  * UK Biobank (UKBB): Yes. The text mentions that UKBB is a large population database that will be compared, supplemented, or integrated with other ongoing large populational sequencing efforts.
  * All of Us Research Program (AoURP): Yes. The text mentions that AoURP is a large population database that will be compared, supplemented, or integrated with other ongoing large populational sequencing efforts.
  * Human Heredity and Health in Africa project (H3Africa): Yes. The text mentions that H3Africa is a large population database that will be compared, supplemented, or integrated with other ongoing large populational sequencing efforts.
  * Centers for Mendelian Genomics (CMG): Yes. The text mentions that CMG is a large family-based genomic sequencing program that has generated multidisciplinary data.
  * Undiagnosed Diseases Network (UDN): Yes. The text mentions that UDN is a large family-based genomic sequencing program that has generated multidisciplinary data.
  * Genomics Research to Elucidate the Genetics of Rare diseases Consortium (GREGoR): Yes. The text mentions that GREGoR is a large family-based genomic sequencing program that has generated multidisciplinary data.
  * Icelandic deCode Project: Yes. The text mentions that the Icelandic deCode Project is a national genome sequencing project that targets the general population.
